channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Was This Really the First Humanoid Robot at the White House? An Investigation
item.description: Suddenly Al Gore doesn't count as "humanoid"?

item.title: 'AI is becoming THE line item': OpenAI and Anthropic are big winners in the doubling of AI spend as legacy SaaS face an existential crisis
item.description: Enterprise AI spend is booming, with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Cursor dominating budgets while legacy SaaS loses relevance and influence.

item.title: Toyota hits the accelerator on EVs as its rivals go into reverse — starting with an $800 million Kentucky plant and three new cars
item.description: Rival automakers are pulling the plug on EVs in the US, but Toyota has other plans.

item.title: Meta Lays Off 700 in Pivot From Metaverse to AI
item.description: "Where possible, we are finding other opportunities for employees whose positions may be impacted.”

item.title: Amazons Big Spring Sale is here and surprisingly good: We found 100+ deals on Apple, DJI, Sony, and Shark
item.description: Amazon's Big Spring Sale is back from March 25 to 31. Shop the best deals on Apple, headphones, TVs, and more.

item.title: The Sonos Ace are a hefty 25 percent for Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale might be a great opportunity to pick up a color ebook reader or an RGB-equipped table lamp, but the pickings are slimmer when it comes to noise-canceling headphones. Sure, the Sony WH-1000XM6 are just over $60 off, though I’d argue the Sonos Ace are a better deal, given they’re […]

item.title: Bring back the joy of buying new tech and toys
item.description: Joybuy brings you brilliant tech deals and same-day delivery

item.title: We picked the best Apple deals from Amazons Spring Sale — save on AirPods, iPads, and MacBooks
item.description: See all the best Apple deals from the Amazon Big Spring Sale, including $50 off AirPods Pro 3 and $250 off M4 MacBook Air laptops.

item.title: Dropouts Dimension 20: City Council of Darkness trailer has Brennan Lee Mulligan going vampire
item.description: Dropout's "Dimension 20: City Council of Darkness" trailer has Brennan Lee Mulligan, Emily Axford, Ally Beardsley, Brian Murphy, Zac Oyama, Siobhan Thompson, and Lou Wilson back in the dome for a new adventure.

item.title: This is probably the first mainstream mini PC that can dual-boot Ubuntu and Windows out of the box — and it even comes with the hottest AI application of 2026
item.description: GMKtec NucBox K13 offers Windows-Ubuntu dual-boot, OpenClaw AI with security flaws, Intel Ultra 7 power, but heat and reliability issues may persist.

item.title: A court just ruled Meta and YouTube ‘negligent’ — social media may never be the same
item.description: Meta and YouTube just lost a landmark social media case in which a jury found them negligent. Now the question is how, if at all, these platforms change?

item.title: The First Clip from ‘Maul: Shadow Lord’ Plays the Hits, Literally
item.description: Like he's coming out at Wrestlemania, Maul's entrance on his new show is perfectly theatrical.

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which starts today and runs through March 31st, the retail behemoth is hoping to lure in would-be shoppers with the promise of steep(ish) savings and discounts on more seasonal, spring-centric items to hold […]

item.title: Palantir CEO Says Only the Neurodivergent Will Survive the AI Takeover
Bernie Sanders Introduces Bill to Pause AI Data Center Construction, Warns of ‘Cataclysmic Changes’
item.description: The progressive lawmaker is introducing a bill calling for a national moratorium on data center construction until new AI regulations are enacted.

item.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
item.description: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.
